---
title: "Monotony and stability analysis"
subtitle: ""
date: last-modified
sidebar: auto
number-sections: false
toc: true
author:
  - Jumbong Junior 

categories: []
tags: [""]
title-block-banner: false
format: 
  html: 
    mainfont: Times New Roman
    fontsize: 1.1em

jupyter: python3

---



```{python}

import pandas as pd

import importlib
import src.categorization.catego as utils

importlib.reload(utils)
from src.categorization.catego import*

from config import data_path, src_path, data_output_path

```


```{python}
# Impoort data from datasets
path_datasets = data_path
data_cleaning_output_path = data_output_path
```


```{python}
# Import data from datasets
train_stability = pd.read_csv(f"{path_datasets}/train_stability.csv")
train_stability.head()
test_stability = pd.read_csv(f"{path_datasets}/test_stability.csv")

oot_stability = pd.read_csv(f"{path_datasets}/oot_stability.csv")
```


```{python}
variables = [
    "person_income",
    "person_emp_length",
    "loan_int_rate",
    "loan_percent_income"
]

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(16, 10))

axes = axes.flatten()

for ax, variable in zip(axes, variables):
    plot_default_rate_ax(
        train_stability,
        variable=variable,
        target="def",
        bins=10,
        ax=ax
    )

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

# Woe of person_income

```{python}
# Woe of person_income
variable = "person_income"
iv_df, woe_df = iv_woe(train_stability[[variable, "def"]], "def", bins=3, show_woe=True)
woe_df

```

```{python}
print(woe_df)
```

```{python}
print(iv_df)
```


```{python}
train_dis = apply_cutoffs_from_woe_table(
    df=train_stability,
    variable="person_income",
    woe_table=woe_df,
    new_col="person_income_dis",
    labels=["1", "2", "3"]

)

test_dis = apply_cutoffs_from_woe_table(
    df=test_stability,
    variable="person_income",
    woe_table=woe_df,
    new_col="person_income_dis",
    labels=["1", "2", "3"]
)

oot_dis = apply_cutoffs_from_woe_table(
    df=oot_stability,
    variable="person_income",
    woe_table=woe_df,
    new_col="person_income_dis",
    labels=["1", "2", "3"]
)

oot_dis[["person_income", "person_income_dis"]].tail()
```

```{python}
tx_rsq_par_var(train_dis,[ "person_income_dis"], "year", "def")
```

```{python}
combined_barplot_lineplot(train_dis, ["person_income_dis"], "def")
```

```{python}
test_freq_by_group(train_dis, ["person_income_dis"] )
```
```{python}
calculate_relative_difference(train_dis, "person_income_dis", "def")
```


```{python}
variable = "person_emp_length"
iv_df, woe_df = iv_woe(train_stability[[variable, "def"]], "def", bins=3, show_woe=True)
woe_df
```

```{python}
print(woe_df)
```
```{python}
print(iv_df)
```
```{python}
train_dis = apply_cutoffs_from_woe_table(
    df=train_dis,
    variable="person_emp_length",
    woe_table=woe_df,
    new_col="person_emp_length_dis",
    labels=["1", "2", "3"]  
)
test_dis = apply_cutoffs_from_woe_table(
    df=test_dis,
    variable="person_emp_length",
    woe_table=woe_df,
    new_col="person_emp_length_dis",
    labels=["1", "2", "3"]  
)
oot_dis = apply_cutoffs_from_woe_table(
    df=oot_dis,
    variable="person_emp_length",
    woe_table=woe_df,
    new_col="person_emp_length_dis",
    labels=["1", "2", "3"]  
)
oot_dis[["person_emp_length", "person_emp_length_dis"]].tail()
```

```{python}
tx_rsq_par_var(train_dis,[ "person_emp_length_dis"], "year", "def")
```

```{python}
combined_barplot_lineplot(train_dis, ["person_emp_length_dis"], "def")
```

```{python}
test_freq_by_group(train_dis, ["person_emp_length_dis"] )
```

```{python}
calculate_relative_difference(train_dis, "person_emp_length_dis", "def")
```


```{python}
variable = "loan_int_rate"
iv_df, woe_df = iv_woe(train_stability[[variable, "def"]], "def", bins=3, show_woe=True)
woe_df
```

```{python}
print(woe_df)
```
```{python}
print(iv_df)
```
```{python}
train_dis = apply_cutoffs_from_woe_table(
    df=train_dis,
    variable="loan_int_rate",
    woe_table=woe_df,
    new_col="loan_int_rate_dis",
    labels=["3", "2", "1"]
)
test_dis = apply_cutoffs_from_woe_table(
    df=test_dis,
    variable="loan_int_rate",
    woe_table=woe_df,
    new_col="loan_int_rate_dis",
    labels=["3", "2", "1"]
)
oot_dis = apply_cutoffs_from_woe_table(
    df=oot_dis,
    variable="loan_int_rate",
    woe_table=woe_df,
    new_col="loan_int_rate_dis",
    labels=["3", "2", "1"]
)
oot_dis[["loan_int_rate", "loan_int_rate_dis"]].tail()
```

```{python}
tx_rsq_par_var(train_dis,[ "loan_int_rate_dis"], "year", "def")
```

```{python}
combined_barplot_lineplot(train_dis, ["loan_int_rate_dis"], "def")
```

```{python}
test_freq_by_group(train_dis, ["loan_int_rate_dis"] )
```
```{python}
calculate_relative_difference(train_dis, "loan_int_rate_dis", "def")
```

```{python}
variable = "loan_percent_income"
iv_df, woe_df = iv_woe(train_stability[[variable, "def"]], "def", bins=3, show_woe=True)
woe_df
```

```{python}
print(woe_df)
```
```{python}
print(iv_df)
```
```{python}
train_dis = apply_cutoffs_from_woe_table(
    df=train_dis,
    variable="loan_percent_income",
    woe_table=woe_df,
    new_col="loan_percent_income_dis",
    labels=["3", "2", "1"]
)
test_dis = apply_cutoffs_from_woe_table(
    df=test_dis,
    variable="loan_percent_income",
    woe_table=woe_df,
    new_col="loan_percent_income_dis",
    labels=["3", "2", "1"]
)
oot_dis = apply_cutoffs_from_woe_table(
    df=oot_dis,
    variable="loan_percent_income",
    woe_table=woe_df,
    new_col="loan_percent_income_dis",
    labels=["3", "2", "1"]
)
oot_dis[["loan_percent_income", "loan_percent_income_dis"]].tail()
``` 

```{python}
tx_rsq_par_var(train_dis,[ "loan_percent_income_dis"], "year", "def")
``` 


```{python}
combined_barplot_lineplot(train_dis, ["loan_percent_income_dis"], "def")    

```
```{python}
test_freq_by_group(train_dis, ["loan_percent_income_dis"] )
```

```{python}
calculate_relative_difference(train_dis, "loan_percent_income_dis", "def")
``` 

```{python}
variables_dis = [
    "person_income_dis",
    "person_emp_length_dis",
    "loan_int_rate_dis",
    "loan_percent_income_dis"
]

print(train_dis[variables_dis].head())
print(test_dis[variables_dis].head())
print(oot_dis[variables_dis].head())
```
